body.text: The Citizens Public Safety Review and Appeal Board (CPSRAB) provides an opportunity for community members to participate in the oversight of the Kalamazoo Department of Public Safety (KDPS). Created by the City Manager pursuant to the authority granted by the Kalamazoo City Charter, the Board receives citizen input on appeals filed after KDPS complaint investigations and can inquire into any conduct of a public safety officer in the absence of a complaint.

The Board serves three primary purposes: building community trust and credibility in the public safety complaint process; providing an independent review of complaint dispositions that citizens choose to appeal; and advising the City Manager on improvements to policies, procedures, and community outreach related to the complaint process.
For more information about the Board, its work, and annual reports, visit the Board's independent website: kalamazoocpsrab.org.

items: Receive copies of PCR complaints (with personal information redacted) upon request
Be updated monthly on the progress of all active complaint investigations by the Office of Professional Standards
Inquire about any complaint investigation, regardless of the Chief's finding, in the absence of a formal appeal
Review the complaint reporting process and recommend improvements to the Chief and City Manager
Monitor the overall fairness of the complaint process for both citizens and public safety employees
Recommend improvements to the City Manager on policies and procedures implicated in the complaint process
Assist the Department of Public Safety in preparing community outreach materials about the complaint process
Establish subcommittees to research and address public safety and community relations issues
Prepare an annual review covering the number and types of complaints, and assess the thoroughness, accountability, and responsiveness of the complaint process
Issue reports on police/community relations, racial profiling, and other community climate concerns as needed
Hold meetings with community leaders and neighborhood associations at least annually
Organize public forums to discuss community climate and develop action agendas with the Public Safety Chief
Work with KDPS and community organizations to anticipate and prevent problems through data analysis and recommendations

body.text: The Board consists of up to 12 members appointed by the City Manager — up to 6 from diverse organizations representing diverse populations and up to 6 at-large members. A quorum requires 51% of active members present.

body.label: Appointment
body.value: All members are appointed by the City Manager. The Chair is named annually by the City Manager; the Vice Chair is recommended by the Chair and approved by the City Manager.

body.label: Term Length
body.value: Members serve 3-year terms, limited to 2 consecutive terms. A 1-year hiatus is required before reappointment.

body.label: Residency
body.value: Full-time City of Kalamazoo residents receive first priority. Non-residents with identifiable interests located in the City receive second priority.

body.label: Time Commitment
body.value: Approximately 5-8 hours per calendar quarter, including monthly meetings, two KDPS ride-alongs or hiring events per year, and two community outreach events per year.

body.label: Disqualifiers
body.value: Members may not hold political office, be a current City employee, work for another law enforcement agency, be a retired or former law enforcement officer, or be an immediate family member of any KDPS personnel.

body.label: Orientation
body.value: Members must attend an orientation session within their first 90 days and attend the Kalamazoo Regional Citizens Academy within the first year of service.

body.text: CPSRAB reviews appeals of complaint findings made by the Chief of Public Safety after investigation by the Office of Professional Standards. The Board may only recommend changes to findings of exonerated, unfounded, or not sustained.

items.title: File a Complaint
items.desc: Submit a PCR complaint with KDPS online, in person, by mail, phone, or email. OPS receives and investigates the complaint.

items.title: OPS Investigation
items.desc: OPS investigates within 45 days. Upon completion, the Chief makes a final finding and notifies the complainant within 10 business days.

items.title: File an Appeal
items.desc: If unsatisfied with the Chief's finding, file an appeal with the City Manager within 15 business days of receiving the decision.

items.title: CPSRAB Hearing
items.desc: The City Manager refers qualifying appeals to CPSRAB. A hearing is scheduled within 30 business days and held within 60.

items.title: Board Recommendation
items.desc: The Board submits a written report to the City Manager within 14 business days recommending to sustain or reverse the Chief's finding.

body.text: Community members are invited to apply for one of up to 12 seats — up to 6 representing diverse organizations and up to 6 at-large. Applications are reviewed by the City Manager's Office; candidates selected to move forward will be notified and may be interviewed before final appointment.

items.title: Review requirements and disqualifiers
items.desc: Must be a U.S. citizen or legal U.S. resident. City of Kalamazoo residents have first priority; non-residents with identifiable city interests are eligible. Ineligible if you are a current KDPS or city employee, work for another law enforcement agency, are a retired or former law enforcement officer, an immediate family member of KDPS personnel, hold or are seeking political office, or are currently on probation, parole, or subject to an active personal protection order.

items.title: Prepare your materials
items.desc: Write a brief statement describing your relevant experience and your reasons for wanting to serve. A background check form is also required as part of the application.

items.title: Submit your application
items.desc: Apply online at kalamazoocpsrab.org/join-cpsrab, by email at cpsrab@kalamazoocity.org, by calling 311 or (269) 337-8000, or in person at City Hall, 241 W South Street.

items.title: Interview and selection
items.desc: The City Manager or their designee may schedule an interview with selected candidates. All applicants will be notified of the outcome. The City Manager makes the final appointment.

items.title: Orientation and onboarding
items.desc: New members complete an orientation session within their first 90 days covering public safety policies, the complaint process, police authority, and use of force. Members must also attend the Kalamazoo Regional Citizens Academy within their first year of service.
